issues: Canonical nodes cover logical deduction and precise symbolic language but omit the explicit source requirements for diagrams and sketching graphs.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as partial mapping; supplement with graph/diagram nodes if the catalog is extended.

issues: Node REAS-PROF-ARGM-SYMB focuses on precise use of symbols, which is narrower than the source requirement for all mathematical language and syntax.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep as closest available node and note the broader scope of language and syntax.

issues: Source applies to interpreting any contextual solution, whereas the mapped node REAS-MODL-MODL-OUTP is framed around model outputs.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as closest available node; consider broadening the annotation.

issues: Root node NUMM is very broad for the specific statement that numerical methods permit solution to a required level of accuracy.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep as a broad placeholder or add a finer node for numerical approximation concepts.

issues: Node REAS-PROB-PSOL-ESTM captures reasoned estimates but not the required evaluation of accuracy or limitations of solutions.
pointReviews.recommendation: Annotate that the mapping covers only part of the requirement.

issues: No canonical node represents the full problem-solving cycle as described in the source.
pointReviews.recommendation: Leave unmapped or create a dedicated problem-solving-cycle node.

issues: Mapping to REAS-MODL-MODL and REAS-MODL-MODL-ASSM does not fully capture refining models by considering outputs or evaluating model appropriateness.
pointReviews.recommendation: Annotate partial coverage or add nodes for model refinement and evaluation.

issues: Node set covers the main techniques but does not explicitly map the overall framing of working with quadratic functions and their graphs or calculator use.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as partial mapping; no better node set is available.

issues: Mapping covers linear, quadratic, set-notation and graphical inequalities but omits inequalities with brackets/fractions and the dotted/solid line convention.
pointReviews.recommendation: Annotate the omitted details or add a dedicated inequalities-techniques node.

issues: Mapping covers the factor theorem and division by linear expressions but omits expanding/collecting like terms and the simplification of rational expressions by factorising and cancelling.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add ALGF-EXP-MANI or a rational-expressions node if available, or annotate the gap.

issues: Mapping covers polynomial, modulus, reciprocal and proportional graphs but does not explicitly include y = a/x^2 or the use of intersections to solve equations.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as partial mapping; supplement with reciprocal-graph detail if the catalog permits.

issues: Individual transformation nodes are mapped but combined transformations and modulus transformations are not explicitly represented.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a combined-transformations node or note the combined cases in the mapping.

issues: Mapping to modelling output and assumption nodes does not explicitly represent the use of specific function families (trigonometric, exponential, reciprocal) in modelling.
pointReviews.recommendation: Annotate the function-family context or link to relevant function/model nodes.

issues: Canonical mapping covers straight-line forms but does not include a node for parallel/perpendicular gradient conditions or straight-line modelling contexts required by the specification.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add nodes for parallel/perpendicular lines and straight-line modelling, or change relation to partial.

issues: Mapping covers circle equations, tangents and the angle-in-a-semicircle property, but the 'perpendicular from centre to chord bisects the chord' property is not explicitly represented.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a node for the chord-bisecting property or expand the existing chord-properties mapping.
Retain corrected eligible allocation across Papers 1, 2 and 3.

issues: Mapping captures binomial expansions and coefficients but omits the link to binomial probabilities, the validity condition |bx/a|<1, and the use of partial fractions noted in the guidance.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add PROB-DIST-BINO and ALGF-POLY-PART-APPL nodes and include the validity/approximation aspect.

issues: Mapping covers term-to-term and nth-term rules but the supplied catalog has no node for increasing/decreasing/periodic behaviour, which is part of the official requirement.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for sequence behaviour/monotonicity and periodicity, or keep the gap documented.

issues: No dedicated sigma-notation node exists; mapping to the generic Series parent is broader than the official point.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a sigma-notation node to the catalog or retain the broader mapping with a documented gap.

issues: Mapped to a generic modelling-outputs node that does not represent modelling with sequences and series (e.g. savings schemes, arithmetic/geometric growth).
pointReviews.recommendation: Map to a sequence-series modelling node or to REAS-MODL-MODL with a note; do not rely on the outputs node alone.

issues: Mapping covers sine/cosine rules, area formula, ambiguous case and radian arc/sector formulae, but does not include unit-circle definitions of sine and cosine for all arguments.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a unit-circle/extended-definition node to capture 'for all arguments'.

issues: The supplied catalog contains no canonical node for small-angle approximations of sin, cos and tan in radians.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create a new canonical node for small-angle approximations and assign it here.

issues: Mapping covers addition and double-angle formulae but the catalog has no node for the a cosθ + b sinθ ≡ R cos(θ±α) / R sin(θ±α) form or geometrical proofs.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a node for R-form (harmonic form) and note the geometrical-proof requirement.

issues: Mapped to a generic proof-by-deduction node and the identities parent; this does not specifically capture proof construction using trigonometric identities.
pointReviews.recommendation: Use a trigonometric-identities proof node if available, or retain with a documented relation of partial.

issues: Mapping covers a^x and e^x graphs but omits the shape distinction for a<1 versus a>1 and the transformed graph y=e^(ax+b)+c mentioned in the guidance.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add nodes for general exponential graph shapes and transformations, or note the gap.

issues: Mapping mixes the derivative rule for e^(kx) with exponential modelling; no dedicated node captures the proportional-rate-of-change justification for exponential models.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a node for exponential model suitability or keep the partial relation documented.

issues: Mapping covers inverse relationship, natural log and equation solving but does not include the graph of ln x or the inverse relationship of ln x and e^x as a graph reflection.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add the ln x graph node if available or document the missing graph aspect.

issues: Mapped to the broader exponential/logarithmic equations node; this is narrower than that node and only covers a^x=b type equations and change of base.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as narrower but ensure the relation value reflects the narrower scope.

issues: Mapped to exponential models only; the supplied catalog has no node for log-log/log-linear plots used to estimate parameters in power-law and exponential relationships.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create a canonical node for logarithmic graph linearisation and assign it here.

issues: Mapping captures exponential growth/decay and generic model interpretation but does not specifically address model limitations/refinements or large-t behaviour.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a modelling-assumptions/limitations node or document the refinement requirement.

issues: Broad set of nodes covers derivative concepts and applications, but includes differentiation-rule nodes (powers, trig) that belong more naturally to 7.2, and does not fully isolate first-principles limits for sin x and cos x.
pointReviews.recommendation: Remove rule-specific nodes to 7.2 and add a dedicated first-principles node if available.

issues: Mapping covers powers, exponentials, logs and trig but does not explicitly include the derivative of a^(kx), which requires a^x ln a.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a node for differentiating general exponential functions a^(kx) or document the gap.

issues: Mapping covers the main applications but is broad; the relation is partial rather than exact because several distinct skills are grouped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as partial; no action unless more granular nodes are required.

issues: Point groups several distinct techniques; ensure the partial relation is retained to reflect breadth.
pointReviews.recommendation: No action required; relation partial is appropriate.

issues: Mapped only to CALC-INTE-CONC-DEFI; the point is specifically about integration as the limit of a sum, for which no dedicated Riemann-sum node exists.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain CALC-INTE-CONC-DEFI as the closest available node, or add a dedicated Riemann-sum/limit-of-sum node to the catalog if precision is required.

issues: Only distinct-linear partial fractions (ALGF-POLY-PART-LINL) is mapped; the specification also requires rational expressions with repeated linear denominators, so ALGF-POLY-PART-REPL is relevant.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add ALGF-POLY-PART-REPL to the canonicalNodeIds for repeated linear denominators.

issues: Mapped only to REAS-MODL-MODL-OUTP; the point also involves identifying limitations of solutions and kinematic links, which would be better supported by additional modelling/differential-equation nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Broaden mapping to include REAS-MODL-MODL-ASSM and relevant CALC-DIFFEQ or CALC-KIN nodes if available.

issues: Mapped only to the NUMM-ROOT parent node, but the point covers the broader application of numerical methods in context and could include numerical integration.
pointReviews.recommendation: Broaden the mapping to include NUMM-NINT-TRAP-AREA or other NUMM-NINT nodes alongside NUMM-ROOT.

issues: Includes GEOM-COOR-CART-DIST, a coordinate-geometry node, alongside the vector position/distance nodes; the vector distance node is sufficient for this point.
pointReviews.recommendation: Consider removing GEOM-COOR-CART-DIST and retaining the vector-specific nodes.

issues: paperApplicability is not-specified; the official specification places Section A Statistics content in Paper 3.
pointReviews.recommendation: Set paperApplicability to fixed Paper 3 for clarity.

issues: paperApplicability is not-specified; probability is part of Paper 3 Section A.
pointReviews.recommendation: Set paperApplicability to fixed Paper 3 for clarity.

issues: paperApplicability is not-specified; probability is part of Paper 3 Section A.
pointReviews.recommendation: Set paperApplicability to fixed Paper 3 for clarity.

issues: paperApplicability is not-specified; probability modelling is part of Paper 3 Section A.
pointReviews.recommendation: Set paperApplicability to fixed Paper 3 for clarity.

issues: paperApplicability is not-specified; statistical distributions are part of Paper 3 Section A.
The discrete uniform distribution is required by the specification but has no dedicated canonical node in the supplied catalog.
pointReviews.recommendation: Set paperApplicability to fixed Paper 3 and add a discrete-uniform node if one becomes available.

issues: paperApplicability is not-specified; the Normal distribution is part of Paper 3 Section A.
pointReviews.recommendation: Set paperApplicability to fixed Paper 3 for clarity.
Source reference and content match. Consider declaring paperApplicability as Paper 3 (Statistics) because the specification places Topic 4 in Paper 3.

issues: STAT-TEST-HYPO-BINO is more directly aligned with section 5.2 (conducting a binomial proportion test); section 5.1 only develops hypothesis-testing language through a binomial model, so this node fit is partial/weak.
The point explicitly excludes calculation of the product-moment correlation coefficient; ensure the PMCC node is interpreted as interpretation-only.
pointReviews.recommendation: Remove or downgrade STAT-TEST-HYPO-BINO for this point; keep the other hypothesis-language and PMCC nodes; declare Paper 3.
Source and node mapping are supported. Declare paperApplicability as Paper 3 (Statistics).
Source and node mapping are strongly supported. Declare paperApplicability as Paper 3 (Statistics).

issues: Ontology granularity is broader than this official point; the limitation is explicitly recorded.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as a documented partial/unmapped representation.

issues: Ontology granularity is broader than this official point; the limitation is explicitly recorded.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as a documented partial/unmapped representation.

overallIssues.issue: Several overarching-theme points are mapped only partially because the canonical catalog lacks dedicated nodes for broad skills such as the full problem-solving cycle, evaluating limitations of solutions, all mathematical language/syntax, and diagram/graph construction in arguments.

Some Pure Mathematics content points use partial node sets that omit minor required elements (e.g., rational-expression simplification, y = a/x^2 graphs, combined transformations, and function-family modelling contexts).

The specification assigns all Pure Mathematics content to Papers 1 and 2 but does not allocate individual topics to a single paper; the coarse Paper-1/Paper-2 assignment is therefore supported only at the content-strand level.

The supplied canonical catalog lacks nodes for several required topics, leading to unmapped, weak or acceptable mappings: small-angle approximations, sigma notation, a cosθ + b sinθ R-form, log-log/log-linear plots, monotonic/periodic sequence behaviour, unit-circle definitions, chord-bisect property, transformed exponential graphs, derivative of a^(kx), and detailed model limitations/refinements.

Fixed paper allocations for Pure Mathematics sections are broadly consistent with the Paper 1/Paper 2 structure, but the official text only states that Paper 1 and Paper 2 may contain questions on any Pure topics; individual sections are not explicitly allocated to papers.

Several Statistics and Probability points (2.4, 3.1-3.3, 4.1, 4.2) leave paperApplicability as not-specified, even though the official specification places all Paper-3 Section A Statistics content in Paper 3. This does not conflict with the source but reduces precision.
OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9MA0-9.5 is mapped only to the NUMM-ROOT parent node; the point covers the broader application of numerical methods in context and could include numerical integration (NUMM-NINT).

OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9MA0-8.6 maps only distinct-linear partial fractions; the specification also requires integration of rational expressions with repeated linear denominators, so ALGF-POLY-PART-REPL is relevant.
OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9MA0-8.4 and OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9MA0-8.8 have comparatively narrow mappings because the catalog lacks dedicated Riemann-sum and differential-equation-interpretation nodes.
OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9MA0-10.4 includes a coordinate-geometry distance node (GEOM-COOR-CART-DIST) alongside vector nodes; the vector distance node is sufficient for this point.
No fixed paper allocation is declared for any of the four points, although the official specification places Topic 4 and Topic 5 in Paper 3: Statistics and Mechanics.
STAT-TEST-HYPO-BINO is used in the mapping for section 5.1, where the specification only develops hypothesis-testing language through a binomial model; the actual binomial test is section 5.2.

Inconsistent identifier prefixing across the candidate index: overarching themes and Pure Mathematics Topics 1–5 use the 'OFFICIAL-9MA0-' prefix, while Pure Mathematics Topics 6–10 and all Statistics content use the 'OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9MA0-' prefix. This naming fragmentation complicates lookup, merging and consistency checking.
